title: Energy and Environment
description: RAND energy and environmental analyses examine the implications of existing and proposed energy policies on the environment. Building on a long history of policy research, RAND helps balance the need for environmental protections and economic development.
text:
Sun-sational Incentives: Residential Solar in the Golden State
This analysis of the impact of income on the effectiveness of solar incentives in California suggests that incentives yield greater installation rates and capacity in higher-income areas than in less affluent ones—a key insight for incentive policies.
Equitable Water Management: A Practical Guide for Utilities
This guidebook was developed in response to growing recognition within the water sector that water services are not equitably distributed.
Why Korea's Nuclear-Powered Submarine Matters to U.S. Strategy
A nuclear-powered submarine would not just be a Korean capability upgrade. In an era of intensifying undersea competition and growing demands on U.S. forces, this is a strategic opportunity for the alliance, as well as for security in the Indo-Pacific.
Robust Conservation Planning for Biodiversity Under Climate Change Uncertainty
Our conceptual study seeks the strengths and vulnerabilities of each strategy across many possible future directions, facilitating both decision-making amongst strategies and emergence of robust and adaptive conservation plans.
Supporting Climate-Related Decisions Under Uncertainty
The chapter demonstrates an RDM approach to identifying a robust mix of policy instruments—carbon taxes and technology subsidies—for reducing greenhouse gas emissions.
A Systemic Risk Assessment Methodological Framework for the Global Polycrisis
By assessing two recent global food and energy systemic crises, we propose a methodological framework applicable to assessing systemic risks in a polycrisis context, drawing from and building on existing approaches.
Venezuela After Maduro: Q&A with RAND Experts
Many questions remain one month after the dramatic U.S. operation that captured Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro. How will sudden change reshape Venezuela's future? What are the regional impacts? And how might the U.S.-Venezuela relationship evolve?
China Could Play Spoiler in Venezuela's Debt Restructuring
Venezuela's external liabilities likely exceed $150 billion. Absent a credible debt restructuring, sanctions relief and higher oil output will not translate into durable stabilization or sustained investment. And China's oil-backed loans give it leverage to delay a comprehensive debt restructuring.
Facing the Flames: Harnessing Technology to Combat Wildfire
Fire chiefs and private-sector leaders discuss how wildfire technology innovations can move from idea to field use and identify gaps in the innovation process. The video highlights how technology can improve wildfire management and resilience.
Building a Smarter Wildfire Innovation Ecosystem
Wildfires in the United States have become more frequent and more destructive. New approaches to wildfire management, driven by innovative technologies, could help make catastrophic fires a thing of the past.
